prefer arbitrary precision numbers hs bad int good integer in general you should prefer arbitrary precision lossless numbers rather than fixed precision lossy numbers instead of why not int integer word natural double float rational you should only use the fixed precision types when you can prove that they actually make a positive impact on performance either cpu time or ram usage fixed precision types are also fine when interacting with external systems that use them like for example postgresql

set data split shuffle to false by default when fold strategy is set to timeseries describe the feature you want to add to this project currently you can split data in time series manner by setting fold strategy to timeseries in pycaret regression setup however data split shuffle is true by default which can lead to timeseries split on randomly ordered data if a user doesn t explicitly set data split shuffle to false python probably not what users want s setup data target fold strategy timeseries correct s setup data target fold strategy timeseries data split shuffle false i don t think there is a use case for timeseries split on randomly ordered data as users are expected to sort data by date before passing to the function there is a risk when users forget to set data split shuffle to false as it can lead to unexpected data split and possibly harm model evaluation result thus i think it is safer to limit data split shuffle to be only false when fold strategy is timeseries describe your proposed solution i want to add the following python if fold strategy timeseries and fold shuffle is true raise valueerror timeseries fold strategy requires data split shuffle to be false as it can lead to unexpected data split describe alternatives you ve considered if relevant no response additional context i did not notice this behavior myself and realized later that my model evaluation was too optimistic as the split was done randomly when i expected to be time series i think this proposition is a change that contributes to more successful application of ml to real world problems by reducing the unexpected mistakes

fix the cookie redirect describe the bug when a user is authenticated by google the current login process will redirect back to the referrer url tracked from the incoming login request this makes the login token cookie only available to the personal data server domain but not the edge server so it is impossible to send the login token to the edge proxy api solution by looking at this redirect should occur in two stages the edge proxy allocates a special url space e g host redirect which is directs the pds sso to redirect into which then has the function of capturing any session token and then issuing a final local redirect back to host

google drive inversion of a specific image is different between two devices bug report website address bug description i logged into my ff account in a new device and so it synced all my add ons and their settings i didn t mess around in dr s settings after this either i went to google drive and noticed that the image that appears in the details tab when there are no files selected was darkened i found out that it was because of the div div div svg path selector and removing it fixed it however as i still had access to my old device i opened google drive on it and found out that it looked normal removing the previously mentioned selector instead made the image dark like it was in the new device by default when opening devtools i realized that in the new device it said the value of its style attribute was darkreader inline fill while in the old device it was darkreader inline fill this last value was also present in a new profile and google chrome steps to reproduce expected behavior consistent behaviour between both devices actual behavior inconsistent behaviour between both instances screenshots the image in question properly inverted old device new device system information specify the browser name and version as well as the dark reader version you are using please do an online search for help if you are not familiar with how to get this information os windows browser firefox dark reader version additional context a fix would be simply adding to the end of that selector but i was not entirely sure if it could indicate a bigger problem or if it was just a weird bug on that specific profile or just a setting i somehow missed

hydrobase installer distribute a tool with tstool to install hydrobase in discussions with doug stenzel the topic came up of what to do about the hydrobase database manager tool apparently the source code is no longer available and the tool is not actively maintained if there is a problem it will not be possible to troubleshoot the program doug has indicated that rather than distribute a tool documentation can be provided to tell power users how to install sql server and attach a database this is probably the bare minimum that could be done another option would be to distribute a windows cmd file or similar to step users through the steps this could use a simple text menu i have done such things for other tools although i generally use bash linux shell such as with search for runinteractive function the nice thing about a script is that it forces users to go through the known steps and feedback can be provided by resting each step i have created such tools for simple to complex environments a tool like this could be distributed with tstool so people would just need to download and install tstool and then run the script my main concern is whether it is possible to automate setup but i assume so since the original tool must have called sql server command line to install addressing this ahead of time would allow for a clean transition from the legacy tool rather than figuring out in a crisis

add end to end tests to the searchchannelcmdf mmctl command mattermost is increasing the end to end test coverage for the the goal is to create a suite of tests that check the main logic paths of each command and ensure that they work against a running server this help wanted issue covers the addition of end to end tests to the searchchannelcmdf command in the commands channel go file the expected way to add these tests is to create a test function in the commands channel test go file and add one s run block for each possible test case thanks to the good amount of already existing unit tests that the commands have it is not necessary to write an end to end test for every possible logic path of the command the main focus should be in covering the happy path the error path and the cases where the user doesn t have permissions to perform an action to help with the test process there are some helpers that can be used the s setuptesthelper initbasic chain will create a set of default users teams and channels to be used during the tests s runforallclients is useful when a test case should be valid for a sysadmin user a and an unprivileged user and s runforsystemadminandlocal will test a block for both a sysadmin user and a local mode user please take a look at the example pull request to see how all these work example mattermost mmctl for instructions on how to set up the end to end tests take a look at if you have questions about the ticket or you need any help feel free to post your question in or contact miguel delacruz in if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

add end to end tests to the verifyuseremailwithouttokencmdf mmctl command mattermost is increasing the end to end test coverage for the the goal is to create a suite of tests that check the main logic paths of each command and ensure that they work against a running server this help wanted issue covers the addition of end to end tests to the verifyuseremailwithouttokencmdf command in the commands user go file the expected way to add these tests is to create a test function in the commands user test go file and add one s run block for each possible test case thanks to the good amount of already existing unit tests that the commands have it is not necessary to write an end to end test for every possible logic path of the command the main focus should be in covering the happy path the error path and the cases where the user doesn t have permissions to perform an action to help with the test process there are some helpers that can be used the s setuptesthelper initbasic chain will create a set of default users teams and channels to be used during the tests s runforallclients is useful when a test case should be valid for a sysadmin user a and an unprivileged user and s runforsystemadminandlocal will test a block for both a sysadmin user and a local mode user please take a look at the example pull request to see how all these work example mattermost mmctl for instructions on how to set up the end to end tests take a look at if you have questions about the ticket or you need any help feel free to post your question in or contact miguel delacruz in if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

css variables directly in the css files this ticket is part of a larger campaign how to migrate in the go to line number in the file utils utils jsx you should see the following javascript changecss app body navbar wrapper navbar default navbar brand color theme sidebarheadertextcolor for this ticket we will be replacing the use of the javascript variable theme sidebarheadertextcolor from this line of code with the css variable sidebar header text color please work off the branch campaign applytheme sidebar header text color to avoid conflicts with other tickets for the same campaign run through these steps to determine where to apply the new css variable followed by removing the call to changecss from utils utils jsx make note of the css statement from the first argument to changecss and specifically the final selector eg app body sidebar menu using your browsers dev tools locate the element targeted by the css statement in the webapp with the element selected in your dev tools use the style browser to get an idea of how styles are currently applied to this element this will help you track down where in the source code you will need to add the appropriate css variable in sass layout sidebar menu scss ln make the necessary changes additions to the css to apply the css variable and then remove the call to changecss from utils utils jsx create a pull request with your changes against the branch campaign applytheme sidebar header text color we will be using this branch to review and qa changes in batches on a regular basis instead of on each individual ticket in the campaign given the number of overall tickets for this campaign notes some instances of changecss affect the styles of sections of the ui that might not be visible by default remember to check modals pop ups menus and different interaction states hover active etc as necessary based on the particular ticket you are working on some instances of changecss include the use of a custom changeopacity function when applying colours that need to be partially transparent these instances will need to be replaced with the rgba css function for example changeopacity theme sidebarbg in utils utils jsx would become rgba var sidebar bg rgb in css appending rgb to the css variable name is necessary when using one of the theme variables to work with the rgba css function some ui elements in the desktop web view will actually be different elements in the mobile web view eg the desktop and mobile primary hamburger menus have completely separate markup please be sure to test the primary responsive breakpoint widths after migration desktop tablet mobile please be sure to test changing themes after migration to ensure the new css variable is being applied correctly switching back and forth between light and dark themes would be a good way to help determine this responsive breakpoints should also be tested with different themes it is possible that an existing changecss line is no longer valid in which case just removing the line as part of your pr is a perfectly valid solution example of migrated code this pr includes several commits that each address a slightly different css statement in order to provide several examples of differing situations and how they were migrated we suggest taking a look at each to get an idea of possible differences you could run across and how add end to end tests to the restoreteamscmdf mmctl command mattermost is increasing the end to end test coverage for the the goal is to create a suite of tests that check the main logic paths of each command and ensure that they work against a running server this help wanted issue covers the addition of end to end tests to the restoreteamscmdf command in the commands team go file the expected way to add these tests is to create a test function in the commands team test go file and add one s run block for each possible test case thanks to the good amount of already existing unit tests that the commands have it is not necessary to write an end to end test for every possible logic path of the command the main focus should be in covering the happy path the error path and the cases where the user doesn t have permissions to perform an action to help with the test process there are some helpers that can be used the s setuptesthelper initbasic chain will create a set of default users teams and channels to be used during the tests s runforallclients is useful when a test case should be valid for a sysadmin user a and an unprivileged user and s runforsystemadminandlocal will test a block for both a sysadmin user and a local mode user please take a look at the example pull request to see how all these work example mattermost mmctl for instructions on how to set up the end to end tests take a look at if you have questions about the ticket or you need any help feel free to post your question in or contact miguel delacruz in if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

add end to end tests to the resetusermfacmdf mmctl command mattermost is increasing the end to end test coverage for the the goal is to create a suite of tests that check the main logic paths of each command and ensure that they work against a running server this help wanted issue covers the addition of end to end tests to the resetusermfacmdf command in the commands user go file the expected way to add these tests is to create a test function in the commands user test go file and add one s run block for each possible test case thanks to the good amount of already existing unit tests that the commands have it is not necessary to write an end to end test for every possible logic path of the command the main focus should be in covering the happy path the error path and the cases where the user doesn t have permissions to perform an action to help with the test process there are some helpers that can be used the s setuptesthelper initbasic chain will create a set of default users teams and channels to be used during the tests s runforallclients is useful when a test case should be valid for a sysadmin user a and an unprivileged user and s runforsystemadminandlocal will test a block for both a sysadmin user and a local mode user please take a look at the example pull request to see how all these work example mattermost mmctl for instructions on how to set up the end to end tests take a look at if you have questions about the ticket or you need any help feel free to post your question in or contact miguel delacruz in if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

improve how shorturl relations are resolved domains and tags before opening an issue just take into account that this is a completely free of charge and open source project i m always happy to help and provide support but some understanding will be expected i do this in my own free time so expect some delays when implementing new features and fixing bugs and don t take it personal if an issue gets eventually closed you may also be asked to provide tests or ways to reproduce reported bugs try to be polite and understand it is impossible for an oss project to cover all use cases currently when creating a shorturl there are two entities that could need to be created or fetched as a side effect tag and domain these are always provided as strings that reference to a unique index for their respective entities which result in the tag domain being created if it does not exist or fetched otherwise resulting in a relationship with shorturl in both cases this is currently handled differently for tags and domains for domains the shorturl expects an object implementing domainresolverinterface to be optionally provided if it is not provided it falls back to the basic implementation simpledomainresolver which just returns a new domain instance always however at runtime a persistencedomainresolver is always provided which depends on the entitymanager and therefore it is capable of persisitng fetching actual domains from persistence layer for tags services that need to create the list of tags for a shorturl use the tagmanagertrait which exposes a method to create fetch tags these tags are later set to the shorturl via setter proposal these are the problems we need to resolve lack of consistency between how both entities are handled usage of a trait in the second case requirement of a setter in the second case the way in which this problem is handled for domains is better than the other one but having to pass resolvers for each one of them makes it a bit cumbersome one option is to have a single resolver that can handle both entities and exposes public methods to persist fetch both kinds of entities we would still keep a simple implementation to which the logic falls back for unit tests and such

securing cloudformation custom resource access from workload accounts relies on securing stacksets exec iam roles when using service managed permissions for cloudformation stack sets the following occurs when first configured this service role is configured in the master account arn aws iam xxx role aws service role stacksets cloudformation amazonaws com awsserviceroleforcloudformationstacksetsorgadmin and this service role is created in the member accounts arn aws iam xxx role aws service role member org stacksets cloudformation amazonaws com awsserviceroleforcloudformationstacksetsorgmember when a stack is created via a stack set the awsserviceroleforcloudformationstacksetsorgmember role is assumed by member org stacksets cloudformation amazonaws com in a ss bootstrap session and used to create a role with name pattern stacksets exec the trust policy on this stacksets exec role allows only the awsserviceroleforcloudformationstacksetsorgadmin service role to assume it the awsserviceroleforcloudformationstacksetsorgadmin service role assumes the stacksets exec role and it is used to provision resources as part of the stack this is an issue specifically for little orange because it leverages lambda backed custom resources in the master account that need to be securely invoked from all accounts in the organization the issue here is that if you are attempting to secure resources via iam so they can only be accessed by the service managed permissions of cloudformation stack sets you are limited to targeting the stacksets exec roles via aws principalarn in an iam condition if a user created a role called stacksets exec my malicious role it could invoke the custom resources and therefore modify sensitive state of the organization one partial resolution here is to introduce an scp across the organization that limits the creation and management of these stacksets exec roles to the awsserviceroleforcloudformationstacksetsorgmember service roles

add end to end tests to the movechannelcmdf mmctl command mattermost is increasing the end to end test coverage for the the goal is to create a suite of tests that check the main logic paths of each command and ensure that they work against a running server this help wanted issue covers the addition of end to end tests to the movechannelcmdf command in the commands channel go file the expected way to add these tests is to create a test function in the commands channel test go file and add one s run block for each possible test case thanks to the good amount of already existing unit tests that the commands have it is not necessary to write an end to end test for every possible logic path of the command the main focus should be in covering the happy path the error path and the cases where the user doesn t have permissions to perform an action to help with the test process there are some helpers that can be used the s setuptesthelper initbasic chain will create a set of default users teams and channels to be used during the tests s runforallclients is useful when a test case should be valid for a sysadmin user a and an unprivileged user and s runforsystemadminandlocal will test a block for both a sysadmin user and a local mode user please take a look at the example pull request to see how all these work example mattermost mmctl for instructions on how to set up the end to end tests take a look at if you have questions about the ticket or you need any help feel free to post your question in or contact miguel delacruz in if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

start using snapshot for release upgrade tests in the ci component upgrade tests why this is needed currently in the ci we get the iso and do an install of the released version to then perform an upgrade to the version we try to test several issues some flakies may happen during install of the released version it s not what we want to test here so it takes some time to install this released version on every post merge for one branch we need to support install process of older versions in the ci which is not really good what should be done start using snapshot of working cluster in released versions e g if we want to test upgrade from to dev create some vm s using snapshot wait for the cluster to be fully running and ready upgrade to dev implementation proposal strongly recommended for a first iteration create some snapshot manually for every versions we want to test in the ci adapt or create a new terraform to spawn machine using those snapshot wait for cluster to be fully ready and running upgrade to launch tests note since we rely on etcd we need to take different snapshot depending on the cluster size so let s follow this naming style bootstrap node x for example let s consider a node etcd master workload we will have images bootstrap node node

support billing project in google project service billing project is a configurable project used in user project overrides eg it works automatically for generated resources but doesn t work on handwritten resources where it s not manually added we should support it here this is useful particularly for this resource because service activations rely on the actor credentials having the serviceusage googleapis com service enabled this is always fine for a user account but there s a bit of a bootstrapping problem for service accounts if i create project foo with my user credentials or a privileged sa and then create service account bar within that project i can t later enable services inside the account because bar s project doesn t have serviceusage enabled by setting a project override for that request the override would be checked instead of the project of the service account currently implemented only the direct project the project where the service is enabled can be used as an override

we should merge scratch desktop branch into develop expected behavior the scratch app for macos windows scratch desktop repository should use the normal version of scratch gui from the develop branch actual behavior the scratch app for macos windows scratch desktop repository uses a special scratch desktop branch of scratch gui this branch is periodically updated by merging from develop which sometimes takes quite a bit of effort due to merge conflicts especially when there are changes related to the media libraries this is extra maintenance effort and risk that we could avoid by merging the changes unique to the scratch desktop are in principle harmless or even beneficial to the web version of scratch gui but a few implementation details need to be settled before it s truly safe to merge the biggest hurdle is ensuring that the media libraries load quickly and smoothly in both contexts web browser and electron which may require changes in scratch storage

reconsider write defaults off write defaults is an option available on states in animators in unity the documentation unity provides is a bit sparse and the actual function of this option is a bit cryptic basically write defaults being on will tell the state to write the things it is animating as the new default state of that transform blendshape etc however this causes some significant issues normally when you re working with other game developers on a project you agree on a standard in vrchat we re all game developers so let s set the standard here vrchat does not use write defaults in our built in and example animators this means that only the actual properties that are in animations get played by any one animation node we recommend that creators also follow this workflow as it is easier to keep track of what properties will be animated through any specific layer the write defaults value defaults to on when you create a new node so creators must be aware they will have to uncheck this value if you want to use write defaults you will have to keep track of all the possible properties that may be written by a node with this enabled we recommend keeping write defaults off and explicitly animating any parameter that needs to be set by the animation note that this may require adding reset animations or adding properties to the animation to initialize transforms in a specific orientation

variable followed by removing the call to changecss from utils utils jsx make note of the css statement from the first argument to changecss and specifically the final selector eg app body sidebar blackbox exporter site next steps summary historically we have used to monitor however it has also been the source of some ephemeral alerts that has been cause for concern and reduced faith in its reliability see in an effort to reduce our dependency on and improve our on call experience and our monitoring reliability was deployed at the time of raising this issue each endpoint configured in is replicated in blackbox exporter all of which are configured to alert if a non http status code is not received for minutes site the good be default we get a bunch of useful metrics out of the box without having to configure anything specific aside from the endpoint itself response time broken down into dns resolution download ssl etc the ui is relatively simple to use summary of outages and the rca image is sometimes handy to see where things are breaking down testing from multiple locations cost the bad we get monitoring up until cloudflare which is to say that there s no visibility of external vs internal issues in the same platform as a result it s hard to know if the issue is with cloudflare or with the sourcegraph frontend and where to look next blackbox exporter the good there s a lot of knobs we can turn and tune here to gather exactly the information we find most useful we can easily see on the same grafana dashboard all of the endpoints we have configured per metric we get internal and external monitoring of endpoints out of the same service that is to say we can configure a ping to hit an exernal service as well as the services themselves free the bad there is some intitial setup required in order to determine what metrics we want on which endpoints all of which need to be configured manually in the various config files blackbox exporter and prometheus as well as any further alertmanager config that may be required we essentially only get one source of truth here as we are testing from inside our own cluster out to the world then back again we do not get multiple locations like there will be ongoing maintenance which at this stage is hard to say what that will be however one could assume the more metrics we gather the more we need to maintain it and ensure they re up to date what s next determine what we consider to be missing from blackbox exporter that we currently have in considering the metrics out of the box that are displayed on vs what we can and need to configure in blackbox exporter ensure that alerting is actually working from blackbox exporter and that it s a trustworthy source for example a comparison in the most recent outage reported by is and the same graph time period from blackbox exporter for both the external endpoint as well as the frontend service

inconsistency with data path to auto complete and request parameters in singleselection field type q a sulu version since browser version actual behavior the singleselection field type has two params one is called data path to auto complete which allows to read data using a json pointer from the form and append that information in the query string and the other is request parameters enabling to statically define some parameters to be appended in the query string in the xml template both of these params are collections but the problem is that the meaning of the name and the value of the child parameters is switched so if you want to have a query string like title of page you would have to define it in this way xml this is very confusing because in the request parameters case the name is used for the name of the query parameter whereby in the data path to auto complete case the value is used as the query parameter name the reason for implementing it this way in the data path to auto complete case is that the name of the param is currently a mandatory field but the value is not therefore the name was chosen to be the default for the path in that case to allow a shortcut like this xml then the title query parameter is filled with the value of the current title field however it could also be handled the other way round this is just what makes this a little bit more complicated this problem was introduced in but it was not possible to solve in a different way the only other solution would have been to switch the meaning of the value and name parameters for the request parameters just in this case which would also make it inconsistent with all the other occurences of request parameters expected behavior the only important thing is that the name of the query parameter is determined in a consistent way i would personally prefer to use the name as the name of the parameter and the value as the value passed to that query parameter i think that makes a lot more sense than the other way round xml mind that we also talked about making the name attribute of the param tag an optional field that would also solve some confusions in other parts of the system where only one of both is needed and that now has to be the name steps to reproduce use the first xml file posted above and see that it works but the xml configuration itself is confusing possible solutions switch the meaning of name and value in the but remember that this is a breaking change

getting rid of the exclusionstarted flag in the status we have an exclusionstarted flag in the pendingremovals section of the status which captures whether we have run the exclude command for an instance this is denormalized from the exclusion list in the database itself denormalizing this information is probably more trouble than its worth because it can cause us to get stuck in a place where we believe we have started an exclusion but it never went through i think it s safer to always consult the exclusion list in the database to determine what instances we have excluded

api account counter issue description calling globalstateservice account returns the actual account counter nonce along with balance from global state however this is not the counter value that should be included in a newly submitted tx for a new tx the system expects the counter to match the projected counter value returned by transactionprocessor getprojection which factors in pending transactions if a client naively reads account data from the api then tries to submit a tx it will fail as a result cc noamnelke avive would appreciate your input on this steps to reproduce call globalstateservice account submit a new valid tx to transactionservice submittransaction using the account counter you just got if there are any pending txs from this sender it will fail expected behavior i see a few options return projected counter and balance instead of current values from global state probably a bad idea add a new api endpoint to get projected account data add projected data to the existing account api endpoints

stop depending on openkeychain summary remove openkeychain as the pgp solution of choice and use protonmail s as a native library to implement pgp operations via jni this allows us to be essentially self hosted simplifying some extremely annoying shenanigens we have to pull off to satisfy openkeychain s activity results based api motivation despite what their maintainers have said the project is more or less dead issues aren t being fixed and pull requests are not being reviewed we ve worked around this previously by replacing the api library with our own but it s about time we break this dependency for good we have multiple issues blocked on openkeychain that can be trivially resolved with an in house implementation that we have better control over golang s crypto apis are maintained by who is a very respected name in the field of cryptography ensuring that security related bugs are found fixed on a regular basis which cannot be guaranteed from openkeychain which had its last audit many years ago and is clearly unmaintained as discussed above drawbacks we will have to build out a significant chunk of ui to make this work i currently do not plan to offer the ability to generate keys so that should take off some of the load we will most certainly face backlash from users about having to keep keys in sync at two places quite frankly i do not care by virtue of staying on top of the latest in the field of android security as we have all this time we re already doing better than openkeychain i would much rather have users be mildly inconvenienced occasionally than have breaking bugs all the time this will require at least one maintainer to have working golang proficiency i do not believe we have such a maintainer as of now but i am willing to fill that void the build system will now need to be equipped to build golang binaries there are plugins for gradle that allow golang libraries to be built but it remains to be seen if they can be used to supply binary dependencies to other modules this build system complication will most certainly have us booted off f droid yet again i do not particularly care an android app store that wants to build binaries itself but can t keep up with updates to the official build tool for android does not deserve to block my work in any fashion users who use f droid should be aware that they re buying into a shoddy ecosystem prior art this has not been discussed previously but has been using gopenpgp for a while now with relative success outside that was worked around easily unresolved questions certainly a lot a non exhaustive list of high level concerns is as follows what will the ui look like how does this fit into the onboarding process what subset of openkeychain functionality do we offer

state machine client interaction specification motivation the interaction protocol between the state machine and the client primarily based on tendermint protocol which is fairly clear and well documented at the same time tendermint architecture is rather flexible and doesn t restrict concrete solutions like rpc message formats encoding error handling timeouts etc the fluence also introduces additional layers of interaction logic transaction authentication deduplication and ordering these aspects make the current protocol rather complicated currently there is no single source of some documentation specification describing this protocol there is implementation code only also some interaction cases are not even implemented yet such a situation leaves the protocol unclear and tangled obstructs the understanding of the whole picture of the interaction which is quite important it also slows down the future development of the new interaction features and alternative client implementations proposition to implement the interaction protocol specification it would describe the interactions from different points of view and different levels the client language specific client high level api how to use this api what to expect what guarantees it provides currently such api implemented in blob master client dataengine py tendermint rpc message formats it s primarily a specification for transaction broadcact tx and query abci query rpcs what is request format what response fields info result proof etc mean the state machine abci methods implementation specification how they act depending on the current state and given arguments the specification must describe both normal behavior and various corner cases including client node failures and byzantine behavior the specification must explicitly inform what features are not actually implemented it s supposed that the required specification would be implemented as markdown document s and placed in fluence main repo

migrate components team selector modal module and associated tests to typescript we are starting to migrate the mattermost webapp over to typescript to facilitate better code quality this help wanted issue is to modify all files in components team selector modal not sub directories included and associated test files below is a checklist of items you should be doing to migrate rename the files to their associated typescript extensions ie js to ts jsx to tsx update any components using those to the correct imports fix any errors generated by the typescript compiler you can run make check types to display any errors we are aiming to be as strict as possible for the time being but if you run into any issues where the file cannot be successfully migrated without significant change in other libraries or our tsconfig json please feel free to raise them as we are aiming to keep this process smooth and gradual while enforcing as much as we can some examples of already migrated files can be found here if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

revisit usage of box shadow imo the shadows used in the app are weird here s a notification with its default shadow css box shadow rgba that shadow is unnatural shadows are caused by objects obstructing a light source the object in this case is the notification itself and for that shadow to happen the light source should come perpendicularly out of the notification i e it should be perpendicular to the screen basically it s as if i m the light source in this scenario and i m not usually the main light source in the real world in the real world the biggest light source is the sun and during the day it s pretty much always above the objects obstructing its light rays i e the shadow one sees usually is more accentuated at the bottom not at the top nor it is uniformly distributed like in the screenshot above here s what a notification with a more natural shadow would look like css box shadow rgba rgba rgba i think that s a significant improvement considering we are talking about a shadow enough of an improvement that i had to open this issue 😅

reapproach file checkpointing strategy context the file source was one of our first components and has undergone a number of changes since we created vector this is especially true for the checkpointing feature when we first set out to build the checkpointing feature we were unsure how we wanted to persist a checkpoint s state there were discussions to use leveldb for this since it was being used for our disk buffers but there were also discussions to replace leveldb with something else due to the now moot complexity and performance issues now with the benefit of hindsight we should reapproach our checkpointing strategy to solve issues users are reporting problem the file source checkpointing strategy has the following problems checkpoint files are not cleaned up the individual checkpoint file strategy is not optimally efficient proposals i d like for us to agree on a new direction a couple of ideas to get things started using a single file to store all checkpoint positions this was recommended by anton ryzhov via using leveldb since we seem to be committed to it proposing an alternative solution something specifically designed for persisting state across vector such as sqlite not used for disk buffering obviously

behavior of mergeanym with mergetree and generaltype is ambiguous there is an implicit expectation that mergeanym requires at least one success before passing all successes to mergeany this is useful for mergeable instances that can t differentiate between any and all e g and but it also means that mergeanym will never call mergeany on the other hand the compileinfot implementation of mergeanym collects all errors if there aren t any successes in some cases collectfirstm could be used instead which also collects errors but type checking needs to aggregate guesses using mergeall and mergeany mergeany is expected to be the identity element of mergeany and mergeany is expected to be associative the implementations of mergeanym for maybe and compileinfo have those properties if you only look at error vs the actual content i think that part of the problem is that mergeablem behaves as expected only when mergeall and mergeany are the same operation so the mergeable instances of mergetree and generaltype are more similar to the mergeablem instance of compileinfot so it would almost be better to make mergeablem operate on monoid s and completely avoid mergeable

refactor fetch package thanks for submitting a feature request please fill the template below otherwise we will not be able to process this feature request summary refactor the common pkg fetch why do we need this to allow for fallback to another source i e when a web server goes down another source could be another web location or a local directory or any other source what is already there what do you see now please paste terminal output upload logs as txt or upload screenshots describe or link to related apis screen designs packages etc fetching is currently configured for a single source and the source itself doesn t support a fallback mechanism the http fetcher uses a memory cache transport what is missing what do you want to see we need to specify multiple alternative http locations that are tried sequentially randomly or concurrently we would use this to access geographically distributed or otherwise mirrored data sources that contain the same content we also need to be able to specify multiple layers that are tried for example first any http source then a local directory then a cache with potentially outdated data if the http source is configured with multiple urls all would fail or time out in order to try a fallback layer whenever something gets fetched from the primary layer the cache would be updated keep the compatibility commitment for configuration in mind all configuration is forward compatible how do you propose to test this please think about how this is verified as implemented for example you can add what commands need to be run via the cli console to verify that this works unit testing with multiple http test servers and local testdata dir we need different scenarios tested can you do this yourself and submit a pull request can review

replace cooperative loading of structs with a function or a simple single thread loading many kernels in cuio start with loading a struct to shared memory this is currently done by using all threads to read in a part of the struct in a not reader friendly way examples also it is dangerous as has been rightly stated here increasing the struct size might in some cases cause the whole struct to not be read proposed fix try and replace this with reading using a single thread these reads might not be too expensive as generally there s very few such reads in a block the rest of the computation would easily overshadow it in terms of time spent or replace this with a function that has a descriptive name and handle the overflowing struct size case inside it it could check for the clock size and the struct size and have a loop so read larger structs in smaller blocks

feat handle structure changes in messages api feature description and goals the api users messages api returns the history of tlms that were sent to a user the fields object of those messages are stored as a json column in the database which is currently not migrated if the json schema of any field changes originally this decision of not migrating the fields column has been taken because messages were seen as ephemeral information so losing old ones would not be important such a migration was seen as too complex to do it immediately but now the usages of this messages api increases initially for the notification wall now the activity feed needs it too and we should reconsider how we manage changes in fields ideas brainstorming mode no silly idea the front end ignores messages not conforming to its current version usage pros not too complex to do mainly create front end schemas for the needed structures is desirable in general so that the front end does not blindly fetch what the back end gives see issue ixxx cons would lead to reset all notifications and activities in case of a message change affecting all messages management of the needed schemas is done by hand so prone to forgetting updates on them the front end has code to migrate messages from n current version and ignores older messages pros would avoid resetting notifications activities when a change in messages is made cons more complex than simply maintaining one schema version still loses n and older messages the back end does the migration of the json column pros nothing to do front end wise more classic approach schemas for data written in the json column are available in back end cons manual code for migrations probably needs some helper functions to simplify usual cases change detection is manual so prone to forgetting it the storage is changed to put fields in tables pros migration in back end follows the standard path all sql possibilities for querying indexing messages fields cons initial migration from json multiple tables hard to write more costly changes to messages fields not sure as we have to migrate one way or the other querying messages more difficult needs joining several tables instead of taking the json column

add end to end tests to the botassigncmdf mmctl command mattermost is increasing the end to end test coverage for the the goal is to create a suite of tests that check the main logic paths of each command and ensure that they work against a running server this help wanted issue covers the addition of end to end tests to the botassigncmdf command in the commands bot go file the expected way to add these tests is to create a test function in the commands bot test go file and add one s run block for each possible test case thanks to the good amount of already existing unit tests that the commands have it is not necessary to write an end to end test for every possible logic path of the command the main focus should be in covering the happy path the error path and the cases where the user doesn t have permissions to perform an action to help with the test process there are some helpers that can be used the s setuptesthelper initbasic chain will create a set of default users teams and channels to be used during the tests s runforallclients is useful when a test case should be valid for a sysadmin user a and an unprivileged user and s runforsystemadminandlocal will test a block for both a sysadmin user and a local mode user please take a look at the example pull request to see how all these work example mattermost mmctl for instructions on how to set up the end to end tests take a look at if you have questions about the ticket or you need any help feel free to post your question in or contact miguel delacruz in if you re interested please comment here and come on our daily build server where you can discuss questions with community members and the mattermost core team for technical advice or questions please new contributors please see our jira

bug execution order of tlms handlers description and expectations the activity feed container needs to update an already existing list of activities when tlms are received to decide what it will do it needs the current list of activities and can do some http requests in response to the tlm in order to update the list this work fine most of the time but can fail as seen in cypress tests when two tlms are sent very quickly from the back end in this case the processing of the second tlms can be finished before the processing of the first one leading to missed inconsistent updates in the activities view the same type of race condition can also happen between an http request and a tlm modifying the same structures this problem has been addressed for the activity feed in by serializing the access modifications to the activities list nevertheless the same problem can happen in other parts of the front end as long as the execution of tlm handlers is not serialized or another solution found this issue is here to collect ways of addressing this issue globally and hopefully finding one which is realistic to implement

working with json ld entities is difficult and brittle as a renku graph owner i m happy with the way json ld entities are modelled however using them seems to be pretty difficult and the tests using them brittle the problem the fact is that the current entities modelling in renku is pretty complex the model is reflected on the graph side and it seems to be built in a robust and almost compile time safe manner however the sticking points between the model and the code using it seems to be a bit difficult to maintain and understand what s even worse is that the tests using the model are a bit brittle due to translation from the models used in the tests and the json ld model it seems reasonable that there are sets of implicit non implicit json ld objects required to instantiate more complex json ld structures and so it would be required that the tests would provide translations from its domain models to relevant entities objects acceptance criteria asses if the idea of having json ld entities requiring other entities implicitly or explicitly depending on a case makes sense probably the place which would require the most changes would be bundles object which methods take long lists of arguments with defaults that arguments would have to be changed to entity objects rather than entity objects s properties for instance the bunldes nonmodifieddatasetcommit could be taking a dataset object explicitly and project and activity implicitly change all the usages of the bundles so no entities properties are passed to them but entity objects provide relevant translations from certain domain models to entities models

io work needs to be abstracted out into an internal api currently there are several points in the rd codebase that does some io work e g reading or writing to a file looking at directory etc however none of those various classes should be doing this in fact i d go as far to argue that none of those should ever need to have a using system io and then use the file or directory static class using path is probably ok since we d rather use its string manipulation functions but file directory are definitely not ok in pr we introduced ipersistancepathprovider which provides a common way for getting the root path in pr we got isourcecodehandler which is fairly specialized for handling the source code file we also saw ifilehandler in pr which ultimately did not get merged those address the problems here and there we really need to unify them into a common io api for rd s internal uses so that rd only need to work with relative path generally and not worry about the actual mechanics of io work consolidating all io works into a unified api would also open up avenues for additional features such as the possibility of using named pipes to free us from the temporary file management

unsafe editor resource access scmviewpane background a while ago we introduced canonical resources as a way to solve issues on file systems that are case preserving but case insensitive for example consider files some path file txt some path file txt previously we would open editors which pointed to the same file but with canonical uris we take the first file path into memory and normalize all subsequent ones that only differ in case to that form however this can lead to issues where we we use editor resource e g from the active editor and methods such as isequal or relativepath or isequalorparent because the editor resource will be canonical and may have a different form compared to the one to check against based on whoever first managed to open the editor with that uri e g an extension as a solution we added a iuriidentityservice that can compare canonical uris with others e g iuriidentityservice exturi isequal under the hood this service will ignore casing if the file system provider for the scheme of the uri declares as such this issue is to report that some code is comparing uris from the active editor without taking the uri identity into account if an extension decides to open an editor with casing that is not as expected this code will fail to work please review ps i recently added editorresourceaccessor getoriginaluri and editorresourceaccessor getcanonicaluri as a way to explicitly ask for either the canonical resource of an editor or the original uri the editor was created with once you adopted iuriidentityservice you can safely go back to editor resource which is the canonical form always a use case for getoriginaluri is for example when presenting the uri to a user in a label in that case we never want the canonical form cc jrieken

research simplify access to repository tokens for gitserver for gitserver to be able to clone and fetch repos it needs to get tokens from repo udpater and frontend the way it is currently implemented poses several problems tokens are stored twice in the database once in the external service config and once in the external service repos table tokens are stored on disk by git gitserver is the main service using these tokens yet other services need to maintain logic to get these tokens and pass them around proposal write a type package that provides an unified way of getting a token clone url for a given repo and make all services that need to access the token use that type this type would choose a random external service associated with the repo and read the token from that external service config deprecate drop the external service repos clone url column make gitserver use the new type package instead of calling the other services to get tokens and remove deprecate unecessary endpoints on repo updater and frontend if any spike write a custom to let git ask for a token whenever it s needed this helper would call a gitserver endpoint to get a token on demand this way no token would be stored on disk if this proposal is accepted the bullet points above would be broken down into multiple issues

refactor deferred and blocking return message implementation i did not understand the right way to use xous when i wrote the shell i originally thought to get a non blocking return message one should ask the receiving thread to send a new message in response some time later on this requires disclosing the sender s id which is not possible in xous the right way to do this is to create a new responder work thread for deferred messages and create a unique connection to the service you re looking for a response from and then send a blockingscalar message and have the thread block until the response happens then once the thread receives the return message it passes it back to the main loop using the program s own id so a self to self message this is possible because you do know the sender s id in this case

degenerate nameserver implementation global names are a no no in xous cross process discovery and communication is supposed to be managed by a nameserver unfortunately the nameserver hasn t been written yet so as a bodge we re using well known global names which is sort of the equivalent of using global variables to pass parameters between arguments in c it works but maybe we shouldn t bake that into the initial implementation because we ll regret it later to get around this a degenerate nameserver needs to be implemented even just a mapping of names to ids is ok for now we can resolve the random generation and mapping later but at the very least this allows the server creation routine to have a hook for the correct call to manage names and it will prevent future programmers from relying on well known names which is a feature that will go away eventually

make detail apis order mr parameter after stream describe the bug internal detail namespace apis that mirror public apis often include both memory resource and stream parameters with defaulted values and usually in this order cpp cudf detail example other parameters rmm mr device memory resource mr rmm mr get default resource cudastream t stream however placing mr before stream is somewhat inconvenient because the mr parameter will often use the default because the caller specified memory resource should only be used for allocations that are returned to the caller and not for intermediate temporary allocations if there is no stream in the calling scope the public api can usually be used instead it s less onerous to explicitly specify or nullptr than rmm mr get default resource expected behavior re order the stream and mr parameters of detail apis to make it easier to specify a stream while keeping the implicitly defaulted memory resource also the default value can be removed from the stream parameter to prevent forgetting to hook it up when using the default value for mr cpp cudf detail example other parameters cudastream t stream no longer defaults to rmm mr device memory resource mr rmm mr get default resource additionally the transition md guide could be updated to explain the reasoning for this ordering

remove builtincontext and options arguments from functions most of the haskell functions that implement saw script primitives take two extra arguments of types builtincontext and options some of the functions don t use these arguments at all but none of the functions should need to use them since all of the saw script primitives are implemented in the toplevel monad or another monad layered on top of it and the same information should be obtainable from the monad state passing all these extra arguments around makes the code more complicated harder to read and more error prone as it introduces the possibility of passing arguments that don t match the state in the monad we should get rid of all of them
